key_insight: ReverseMath turns a solved math problem into a new, verifiable one by inverting the answer, creating scalable benchmark and training data.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to generate labeled math data at scale while exposing memorization-like failures in LLMs during evaluation.
method: Mask a numerical value in an original problem.
Use the original answer as a known condition and rewrite the prompt so the masked value becomes the new answer.
Generate paired original/reversed problems for evaluation and RL data augmentation for training.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports substantial behavioral shifts on paired problems, including cases where models fail on reversed items and still output the original answer; adding ReverseMath data improves performance across multiple benchmarks.
watch_item: Answer inversion depends on reliable problem rewriting and may not work well for all math question types or non-numeric reasoning steps.
paper_title: ReverseMath: Answer Inversion for Scalable and Verifiable Mathematical Problem Generation
